title: OpenGATE: Open Grounded AI Testing & Evaluation
description:
An open-source framework for evaluating evidence-grounded AI systems: deterministic scorers, hand-labelled gold sets, and a CI regression gate that measures whether a system can prove its answers from its source material.

Five capabilities behind a small adapter contract: evidence QA, redaction, simplification, retrieval, and generic grounding for RAG. No LLM-as-judge: every scorer is a pure function of (system output, gold case), so results are reproducible bit-for-bit, cost nothing to run, and name the exact expectation violated.

This archived release is the artifact accompanying the preprint Evaluation as Infrastructure: Deterministic Regression Gating for Evidence-Grounded AI Systems in Production. It contains the framework, all gold cases, the committed scorecards from which every number in the paper is derived, the frozen corpus and per-repeat scores for the deterministic-vs-LLM-judge comparison, and the pre-registered blind labelling pack for the independent-labeller experiment.
